basics.name: Hyunseung Hwang
basics.label: Postdoctoral Researcher · NYU Center for Responsible AI
basics.summary: Postdoctoral researcher at NYU's Center for Responsible AI (with Julia Stoyanovich) and a KAIST Ph.D. I stress-test the explanation methods practitioners rely on to audit high-stakes models, exposing their hidden vulnerabilities and engineering more transparent, robust alternatives. My work spans from developing explainability-first clustering frameworks to demonstrating how tools like SHAP are highly sensitive to data-engineering choices. I aim to build trustworthy AI systems where model risk is strictly managed, ensuring that automated decisions in critical domains—from AI alignment to financial services—are safe, accountable, and highly confident.

work.name: New York University (NYU), Center for Responsible AI
highlights: Conducting advanced research on the interpretability and safety of machine learning models under the supervision of Prof. Julia Stoyanovich.
Stress-testing explanation models to ensure safe, confident, and defensible outputs for end-users and stakeholders in high-stakes domains.

work.name: Global Frontier Lab
highlights: Collaborated with Julia Stoyanovich's Responsible AI group at NYU.
Investigated the sensitivity and robustness of AI explainability techniques to deliver safe, confident explanations to stakeholders.

work.name: Naver
highlights: Implemented community detection (Girvan–Newman) on PySpark to cluster unsupervised data for video recommendation.

work.name: Kiswe Mobile
highlights: Built Android features for a live video-streaming service using Android Studio.
Updated the application to meet Android Oreo (8.0+) requirements.

courses: Advisor: Steven Euijong Whang (Data Intelligence Lab)
Dissertation: Enhancing Explainability in Machine Learning — Explainable Clustering and Explanation Multiplicity
